text: We are continuing our Black History Month celebration of people who show us
the power of community, like activist Shirley Reigns, better known as Miss
Shirley, who devoted her life to seeing
the beauty in others. Her activism began
as a way to heal her own trauma from
losing her two-year-old son, but quickly
turned into a viral movement. Miss
Shirley's mission focused on providing
the unhoused in LA Skid Row with
self-worth restoring services. Her
nonprofit beauty to the streets, offered
food, hygiene, beauty services, and what
she called spiritual CPR to the
community. She even learned sign language to communicate with some of the
hearing impaired. When needs of the
unhoused increased after the pandemic,
Miss Shirley went viral on social media,
gathering donations. She gained 7
million followers who helped her feed
thousands and expanded operations to San
Diego, Las Vegas, and Long Beach,
California. She showed the world that
this community was like all of us with
kids, families, many with jobs, but
without a home. She was honored with an
NAACP Image Award and one of CNN's
heroes of the year in 2021.
Miss Shirley passed away last month.
She was 58 years old,
but her legacy proves that the power of
compassion
lives on.
